verdicts.reasoning: The description aligns with the general understanding of Adobe Commerce as a headless commerce platform, which is supported by the homepage content.
The evidence indicates that OAuth 1.0a is supported for third-party applications, contradicting the claim that only oauth2 and token are available.
The evidence supports the claim that developers can create integrations and obtain tokens without human approval on self-hosted instances.
The evidence confirms that both REST and GraphQL APIs are available, indicating broad coverage of product objects.
The ability to self-host and create integrations aligns with the evidence provided, confirming that building integrations is possible today.
